questions.prompt: User mentioned 'exploring Python' once. Months later every code example is in Python — but the user moved to Go. What's the architecture fix?

choices.text: Tag every long-term fact with confidence + source (user-stated, user-confirmed inference, agent inference, behavioral pattern). Expire low-confidence inferences (30-90 days). Re-confirm before treating an inference as a durable fact. Add a 'what I remember' UI so users can correct.

questions.explanation: Confidence tagging + expiration + user review prevents memory poisoning. The original problem was treating a casual inference as a canonical fact. The fix is structural: trust hierarchy in storage, expiration on low-confidence, user controls to correct.

questions.prompt: How should episodic memory be retrieved at session start?

choices.text: Eager-load all of it every session.

Lazy retrieval by embedding similarity to current context (or by topic/time when the user mentions something specific). Eager-load only the small structured layers (profile, lessons learned). Episodic memory can be large; loading all of it costs tokens and pollutes context with irrelevant past content.

questions.explanation: Episodic memory is RAG-shaped: embed past summaries, retrieve top-K relevant to current context. Eager retrieval doesn't scale and pollutes the prompt. Lazy retrieval keeps the prompt focused and the cost bounded.
